features.name: Gemini API Error Handling
features.details: Handles 429 (Quota Exceeded) with 30-hour persistent ban and 503 (Server Busy) with 5-minute cooldown per model/key.

features.name: News Reading
features.details: Optimized with Regex-First extraction and modular Facade architecture.

features.name: Media Button Control
features.details: Supports Headset/Car controls with Mutex-based debouncing

features.name: Resume Reading
features.details: Can resume from last sentence after phone call interruption

features.name: Gemini API Usage Logging
features.details: Detailed logs for API calls (Start, Success, Error, Fallback) with per-model tracking and Gemini-specific UI filter.

features.name: Gemini Client Modularization
features.details: Refactored GeminiApiClient into modular package (com.skul9x.rssreader.data.network.gemini) with GeminiModels.kt, GeminiPrompts.kt, GeminiResponseHelper.kt. All consumer files updated.

features.name: Activity Log Management
features.details: Supports filtering by All, Errors, and Gemini events. Automatically cleans up logs older than 7 days.

patterns.name: Quota Management
patterns.description: Use ModelQuotaManager to track availability of resources (API keys/Models) to prevent wasted calls.

patterns.name: Fire-and-Forget for Non-Critical IO
patterns.description: Use launch() instead of withContext() for database operations that shouldn't block UI/navigation. Example: markAsRead() in skip operations.

patterns.name: Regex-First Extraction
patterns.description: Prioritize Regex over Jsoup for known domains (Genk, VnExpress, Tuoi Tre) to reduce parsing overhead. Fallback to Jsoup if Regex fails.

patterns.name: Facade Pattern for Legacy Refactoring
patterns.description: When refactoring large God classes, create a thin Facade that delegates to smaller components (Strategy, Utils) but keeps the original public API to avoid breaking consumers.

patterns.name: Modular API Helpers
patterns.description: Extract logic for different API responsibilities (Models, Prompts, Response Parsing) into a specialized package to keep clients lightweight.

patterns.name: Unified Synchronization
patterns.description: Always use a single synchronization mechanism (e.g., Mutex) for a shared state to avoid race conditions between Coroutine and Thread contexts.

patterns.name: Batch Operation Chunking
patterns.description: Always chunk large data operations (upload/delete/write) when dealing with external API limits (e.g., Firestore 500-op limit). Chunk at 400 for a safe margin.

gotchas.name: Gemini Thinking Tokens (2.5+ Models)
gotchas.description: Models like gemini-2.5-flash return reasoning in parts[0] by default. MUST iterate through all 'parts' and skip those with 'thought: true' to get actual content.

gotchas.name: Mocking Static Log Methods
gotchas.description: Use mockkStatic(android.util.Log::class) to prevent 'Method not mocked' errors in unit tests checking logs.

gotchas.name: Media Button Blocking
gotchas.description: Avoid using flags for debouncing async media events. Use Mutex.tryLock() with strictly enforced try-finally blocks.

gotchas.name: Service Lifecycle
gotchas.description: startForegroundService can restart service with empty state if process was killed.

gotchas.name: Mutex Deadlock from Suspend Functions
gotchas.description: CRITICAL: Inside a Mutex-protected coroutine, NEVER use withContext() or other suspend functions that might block. Use launch() (fire-and-forget) instead to prevent mutex from being held forever.

gotchas.name: AudioFocus Loss Stops TTS
gotchas.description: TtsManager's AudioFocusChangeListener calls stop() on focus loss events. This can happen unexpectedly during audio stream transitions.

gotchas.name: Android API in Unit Tests
gotchas.description: Classes like android.text.Html or android.util.Log cannot be mocked directly in JVM unit tests. Must use Dependency Injection with Interfaces (AppLogger, HtmlCleaner).

gotchas.name: Short Content Filtering
gotchas.description: Extractors have logic to return null if content < 20-50 chars. Ensure test data exceeds this limit.

gotchas.name: Kotlin Nested TypeAlias
gotchas.description: Kotlin does not support nested type aliases to same-named classes well. Move data classes to top-level if collision occurs during refactoring.

gotchas.name: Mixed Synchronization (Mutex vs @Synchronized)
gotchas.description: CRITICAL: Never mix Coroutine Mutex and JVM @Synchronized on the same shared state. They operate on different levels and don't block each other, leading to race conditions.

gotchas.name: Double-Prompting in Call Chains
gotchas.description: High risk when one helper builds a prompt and passes it to another helper that also bọc (wraps) its input. Ensure raw prompts are only built once.

gotchas.name: Firestore Batch Limits
gotchas.description: Batch writes in Firestore are limited to 500 documents. Exceeding this causes a crash. Always chunk input lists.

gotchas.name: Silent Failure in Sync
gotchas.description: Catching exceptions and returning empty results in sync repositories prevents the coordinator from knowing a retry is needed. Always rethrow after logging.

gotchas.name: Earliest Wins Conflict Resolution
gotchas.description: For 'Read Status' sync, the earliest timestamp is the source of truth (the moment the user first read the item). Logic must use `remote < local` comparison.

conventions: Always add DebugLog before/after mutex operations
Use AppLogger interface instead of static Log calls for testability
Use HtmlCleaner interface instead of static Html.fromHtml for testability
Chunk all Firestore batch operations at 400 items
